key_insight: The paper introduces second-order PFEMs for surface diffusion that preserve enclosed area in 2D and volume in 3D exactly by using Simpson--Boole geometric identities along a quadratic-in-time interpolation path.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical structure-preserving time integrator for evolving curves and surfaces, improving long-time geometric fidelity without adding Lagrange multipliers.
method: Construct a quadratic temporal interpolation and derive exact geometric variation identities.
Use Simpson’s rule in 2D for exact area preservation and Boole’s rule in 3D for exact volume preservation.
Assemble the schemes on BGN-predicted auxiliary geometries to remain compatible with second-order BGN implementations.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments in the abstract report second-order accuracy, exact area/volume conservation, and good mesh quality for both 2D curves and 3D surfaces.
watch_item: The method depends on quadratic-time geometric identities and BGN-based auxiliary geometry assembly, so implementation complexity and compatibility with existing code must be handled carefully.
paper_title: Second-Order Area/Volume-Preserving PFEMs for Surface Diffusion via Simpson--Boole Geometric Identities
